root_text.an6.105:1.1: “Tayome, bhikkhave, bhavā pahātabbā, tīsu sikkhāsu sikkhitabbaṁ.
root_text.an6.105:1.8: Yato kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno ime tayo bhavā pahīnā honti, imāsu ca tīsu sikkhāsu sikkhitasikkho hoti—
root_text.an6.105:1.9: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, bhikkhu acchecchi taṇhaṁ, vivattayi saṁyojanaṁ, sammā mānābhisamayā antamakāsi dukkhassā”ti.

translation_text.an6.105:1.1: “Mendicants, you should give up these three states of existence. And you should train in three trainings.
translation_text.an6.105:1.2: What are the three states of existence you should give up?
translation_text.an6.105:1.3: Existence in the sensual realm, the realm of luminous form, and the formless realm.
translation_text.an6.105:1.4: These are the three states of existence you should give up.
translation_text.an6.105:1.5: What are the three trainings you should train in?
translation_text.an6.105:1.6: The training in the higher ethics, the higher mind, and the higher wisdom.
translation_text.an6.105:1.7: These are the three trainings you should train in.
translation_text.an6.105:1.8: When a mendicant has given up these three states of existence and has trained in these three trainings
translation_text.an6.105:1.9: they’re called a mendicant who has cut off craving, cast off the fetters, and by rightly comprehending conceit has made an end of suffering.”
